claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have a child, Alex, who's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Thank you for that information. 

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask: Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent. And do you live with Alex, or do you contribute financially to their upkeep (such as for food, clothes, or pocket money)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex doesn't live with me, but I do contribute financially towards their upkeep - I help out with things like clothes and other expenses.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine your eligibility, I need to understand a bit more about your contributions. 

Approximately how much do you contribute towards Alex's upkeep each month (or week), and is this contribution at least equal to the Child Benefit amount? (For reference, Child Benefit is currently £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child, which is about £110.93 per month.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I contribute about £26 a week towards Alex's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. One more important question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex (such as the person Alex lives with)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

- **Alex meets the age requirement** - at 8 years old, Alex is under 16
- **You are considered responsible for Alex** - even though Alex doesn't live with you, you're contributing £26 per week towards their upkeep (clothes and other expenses), which is more than the Child Benefit amount of £25.60 per week for an eldest or only child
- **You live in the UK** - which is a requirement for eligibility
- **No one else is claiming** - since nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, you can make the claim

You can proceed with applying for Child Benefit. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so meets the age requirement. Although Alex does not live with the claimant, the claimant is contributing £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep for things like clothes and other expenses. This contribution is at least equal to the Child Benefit amount for an eldest or only child (£25.60 per week). According to the rules, you're usually responsible for a child if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after them (for example on food, clothes or pocket money). The claimant lives in the UK, and nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. Although Alex does not live with the claimant, the claimant contributes £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which exceeds the Child Benefit threshold (£25.60 per week for the eldest/only child). The claimant lives in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

The claimant lives in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
